reviewFocus: Check whether the context pack gives a downstream reviewer enough bounded evidence to act without broad rediscovery.
Flag missing or ambiguous evidence registry entries, copied-truth risk, unsupported readiness claims, and weak continuation boundaries.
Treat the omitted research-source audit as intentional synthetic scope; do not infer that source freshness, retrieval coverage, or approval checks already happened.
Check that the pack remains a caller-owned context handoff rather than a package-owned memory store, tracker mirror, or routing policy.
Keep private source retrieval, source interpretation, approval, action routing, retention, and domain decisions caller-owned.
outOfScope: Do not infer private source contents, real tracker state, account details, or production readiness.
Do not decide publication, deployment, remediation ownership, approval, retention, scheduling, or real-world action.
Do not treat synthetic evidence labels, source handles, or registry rows as proof outside this fixture.
